Official Jitsi Meet documentation (https://jitsi.github.io/handbook/docs/dev-guide/dev-guide-configuration/) confirms p2p: { enabled: false } as correct syntax; export_result.sh lines 24, 33 validate this string in config files; Jitsi Meet base config.js (GitHub) shows p2p object structure; 'config.p2p = {...};' is the JavaScript assignment syntax used in custom-config.js override files

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Jitsi Meet (real open-source video conferencing software, deployed via Docker using stable-9753 images) to disable Peer-to-Peer connections by modifying /home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js with the setting 'config.p2p = { enabled: false };'. This forces video traffic through the Jitsi Videobridge (JVB) instead of direct P2P connections. The configuration is verified by checking the HTTP endpoint at http://localhost:8080/custom-config.js. Evidence is captured as a screenshot showing two participants (Doctor and Patient) in a test room named 'Telehealth-Test' with the connection status popover displaying 'JVB' or 'Bridge' (not 'P2P'). The test scenario is synthetic (hypothetical task), but the underlying software and configuration mechanism are real.
